title: Rapid Response Team Activation on Surgical Floor for 67-Year-Old Man Post-Op Day 2 After Laparoscopic Sigmoid Colectomy with Decompensation to Septic Shock from Anastomotic Leak: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 1-Hour Bundle, RRT Role Assignments + Closed-Loop Communication, Norepinephrine + Vasopressin + Hydrocortisone for Refractory Shock, and Emergent Return to OR for Source Control
subtitle: POSTOPERATIVE ANASTOMOTIC LEAK = catastrophic surgical complication after colorectal resection (incidence 3-15%; HIGHER with low rectal anastomosis + emergency surgery + obesity + diabetes + steroids + immunosuppression + smoking + radiation + male sex); typically presents postoperative day 3-7 (this patient POD 2 — early end); clinical features = fever + tachycardia + abdominal pain + ileus + decreased UOP + hemodynamic instability progressing to septic shock if not recognized + treated; CHARGE RN-LED RAPID RESPONSE TEAM (RRT) activation when ward patient meets criteria (NEWS2 score ≥7, SIRS criteria + qSOFA ≥2, deteriorating vitals, change in mental status, RN concern); RRT TEAM composition = critical care RN + respiratory therapist + ICU intensivist + pharmacy + primary RN + charge RN + house officer/resident; 7 ROLES assigned in 60 seconds — TEAM LEADER (intensivist or senior RN), AIRWAY (RT), CIRCULATION (RN1 + RN2), DOCUMENTATION (charge RN or scribe), MEDICATION (pharmacy or RN3), FAMILY LIAISON (CNS or chaplain), RUNNER (UAP for supplies); CLOSED-LOOP COMMUNICATION (state task + state name + acknowledge + report completion); SBAR HANDOFF; SURVIVING SEPSIS CAMPAIGN 1-HOUR BUNDLE for septic shock per 2021 guidelines = (1) MEASURE LACTATE remeasure if >2; (2) OBTAIN BLOOD CULTURES before antibiotics; (3) ADMINISTER BROAD-SPECTRUM ANTIBIOTICS within 1 hr of recognition (Hour-1 bundle); (4) BEGIN RAPID CRYSTALLOID 30 mL/kg IV for hypotension or lactate ≥4; (5) APPLY VASOPRESSORS if hypotension persists during or after fluid resuscitation to maintain MAP ≥65; NOREPINEPHRINE first-line vasopressor (alpha-1 agonist with modest beta-1 inotropy; preferred over dopamine — less arrhythmia + better mortality outcomes per CATS trial); ADD VASOPRESSIN 0.04 unit/min (FIXED dose, not titrated) when norepinephrine reaches 0.25-0.5 mcg/kg/min and MAP still <65 (catecholamine-sparing + vasodilatory shock physiology); ADD EPINEPHRINE 0.05-0.5 mcg/kg/min for refractory shock with low cardiac index (inotropic support); HYDROCORTISONE 200 mg/day IV continuous infusion or 50 mg q6h for refractory shock not responsive to norepinephrine + vasopressin within ~6 hr (per ADRENAL + APROCCHSS trials — modest mortality benefit + faster shock reversal); SOURCE CONTROL via emergent imaging (CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast) + surgical intervention (return to OR for anastomotic leak washout + diverting ileostomy + drain placement); DELEGATION DECISIONS - 5 RIGHTS (right task + right person + right circumstance + right communication + right supervision); RN-specific tasks (assessment, IV antibiotic initiation, vasopressor titration, communication with team) vs LPN/LVN tasks (stable medication administration, dressings, vital signs) vs UAP/CNA tasks (vital signs in stable patients, ADLs, supply transport); CHAIN OF COMMAND for any concerns or refusal to escalate appropriately; FAMILY-centered communication with informed consent for emergent surgery + transfusion + risks + benefits; UPDATED handoff documentation + SBAR + chart for receiving ICU team

labs.v: 67 y/o male T. Anderson, post-operative day 2 from elective laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy with primary anastomosis for chronic recurrent diverticulitis. PMH: HTN (lisinopril 20 mg + amlodipine 5 mg + hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg — controlled), Type 2 DM (metformin 1000 mg BID + glipizide 5 mg — HbA1c 7.2), hyperlipidemia (atorvastatin 40 mg), occasional NSAID use for OA, no smoking × 25 years (former 40 pack-yr), no alcohol abuse, no chronic steroid use. SOCIAL: married 42 years to wife (65, retired); adult daughter (39, RN at outside hospital — supportive medical literacy); adult son (35, engineer, lives nearby); retired commercial real estate broker × 8 months. SURGICAL HISTORY: appendectomy 1985, knee arthroscopy 2010, cataract surgery 2022. PRE-OPERATIVE EVALUATION uncomplicated — anesthesia clearance + cardiology stress test 3 weeks ago normal + surgical clearance. PROCEDURE: laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy with stapled anastomosis (sigmoid colon resected + anastomosis with EEA stapler) + 3 trocar sites + Foley + epidural for postoperative pain; operative time 3.5 hr; EBL 150 mL; uncomplicated technical course; surgeon technique sound. POD 1 uneventful: HR 76-88 + BP 118-128/72-84 + RR 18-20 + SpO2 96% RA + T 37.2-37.6 + adequate pain control with epidural + ambulating + bowel sounds present + tolerating clear liquids + UOP 60-80 mL/hr. POD 2 MORNING (0700): HR 94 (baseline 76 — 23% above; PRE-SIRS criterion — HR >90); BP 118/68; RR 20; SpO2 96% RA; T 37.8°C (low-grade fever); patient reported mild new nausea + decreased PO intake + 'didn't sleep well' + mild new abdominal discomfort; surgical resident notified; thought to be early atelectasis vs early SIRS vs ileus + continued conservative management; routine labs sent. POD 2 LUNCH (1200): HR 108 + BP 108/64 + RR 22 + SpO2 94% + T 38.2°C; decreased PO + emesis × 1 + abdominal distention + diminished bowel sounds + pain 6/10 (UP from 3/10) + UOP 30 mL/hr (DECLINING from 60-80 baseline; trending TO 25 mL/hr); SIRS criteria met (T >38 + HR >90 + RR >20); RN concern + bedside reassessment + nursing consult to surgical resident; surgical resident clinical exam = abdomen mildly distended + tender + peritoneal signs developing + diminished bowel sounds + concerning trend. POD 2 RAPID CHANGE (1430): HR 128 + BP 84/52 + RR 28 + SpO2 92% on RA → 95% on 2L NC + T 38.8°C + CONFUSION + disorientation + cool peripheries + capillary refill 4 sec + thready pulses + lactate 4.2 + UOP <20 mL/hr × 2 hr + abdomen rigid + peritoneal + significantly distended; PRIMARY RN identified critical deterioration + called CHARGE RN + initiated RAPID RESPONSE TEAM ACTIVATION at 1430 per institutional protocol; activation criteria met: NEWS2 score ≥7 (HR 3 + RR 3 + SpO2 2 + temp 1 + level of consciousness ~4 = 13); SIRS criteria all 4 met (T + HR + RR + WBC pending); qSOFA ≥2 (SBP <100 + RR ≥22 + altered mental status); hemodynamic instability + altered mental status + RN concern — all valid criteria.
labs.r: POST-OPERATIVE ANASTOMOTIC LEAK with progression to septic shock is a CATASTROPHIC surgical complication requiring rapid recognition + escalation. POD 2 is on the early end of typical presentation (usually 3-7 days). EARLY SIGNS at 0700 (low-grade fever + tachycardia) preceded ACUTE DETERIORATION at 1430 (septic shock + altered mental status + acidosis + oliguria). PRIMARY RN recognition + escalation + RRT activation = standard appropriate response. NEWS2 + SIRS + qSOFA criteria objective triggers for RRT.

labs.n: RAPID RESPONSE TEAM (RRT) activation + team roles + communication
labs.v: RAPID RESPONSE TEAM (RRT) activation criteria typically include any of: (1) NEWS2 score ≥7 (National Early Warning Score 2 — composite of HR + RR + SpO2 + supplemental O2 + temp + SBP + level of consciousness); (2) SIRS criteria + qSOFA ≥2 (T >38 or <36 + HR >90 + RR >20 or PaCO2 <32 + WBC >12k or <4k or >10% bands; qSOFA = SBP <100 + RR ≥22 + altered mental status); (3) MEWS (Modified Early Warning Score ≥5); (4) Significant change from baseline vital signs (HR/BP/RR/SpO2/temp); (5) New onset altered mental status or confusion; (6) Decreased UOP <30 mL/hr × 2 hr; (7) Chest pain or new dysrhythmia; (8) Stroke symptoms; (9) Seizure activity; (10) RN concern even without specific criteria — empowered to activate. RRT ACTIVATION procedure: PHONE call to RRT phone number or pager + CODE called over PA system + specific room number + brief reason; primary RN STAYS with patient + initiates immediate stabilization while awaiting team. RRT TEAM COMPOSITION + ROLES (typical 6-8 members): (1) TEAM LEADER = intensivist or senior critical care RN/CNS — overall coordination + decision-making + closed-loop communication; (2) AIRWAY = respiratory therapist — airway management + supplemental O2 + intubation prep; (3) CIRCULATION 1 = critical care RN — central role + IV access + medication administration; (4) CIRCULATION 2 = primary RN — continuity of patient knowledge + bedside care + family; (5) DOCUMENTATION = charge RN or designated scribe — real-time event timeline + interventions + responses + medications + decisions; (6) MEDICATION = pharmacy or RN3 — drawing up + verifying + administering medications; (7) FAMILY LIAISON = CNS or chaplain — communicating with family + emotional support; (8) RUNNER = UAP/CNA — supplies, transport, blood bank, lab, radiology. CLOSED-LOOP COMMUNICATION = (a) team leader states task with specific person ('R. Lee, please intubate now'); (b) person acknowledges ('intubating now'); (c) person reports completion ('intubation complete + ETT secured at 22 cm'); reduces error + ensures task completion. SBAR HANDOFF (Situation + Background + Assessment + Recommendation) standardized communication format; effective in RRT calls + handoffs across units + shifts. POST-RRT DEBRIEFING immediate (within 30 min after stabilization) + formal review at 24-48 hr; identifies system improvements + recognition gaps + team effectiveness + learning opportunities; just culture approach. DOCUMENTATION includes detailed timeline + interventions + responses + team members + outcomes + post-event review; entered in chart + RRT registry for quality improvement + institutional learning.
labs.r: RAPID RESPONSE TEAM activation + structured team approach with assigned roles + closed-loop communication + SBAR handoff are evidence-based practices for managing acute deterioration in hospitalized patients. RRT decreases unanticipated ICU transfers + cardiac arrests + mortality. RN role: recognize early warning signs + activate RRT empowered + stabilize while awaiting team + participate in coordinated response + handoff + debrief.

labs.v: SURVIVING SEPSIS CAMPAIGN 1-HOUR BUNDLE per 2021 guidelines (compressed from previous 3-hr + 6-hr bundles) for sepsis or septic shock recognition: (1) MEASURE LACTATE LEVEL; remeasure if elevated >2 mmol/L (lactate clearance is prognostic marker; >4 = severe + hyperlactatemia + tissue hypoperfusion); (2) OBTAIN BLOOD CULTURES BEFORE administering antibiotics (when possible without delaying antibiotic >45 min — antibiotics within 1 hr more important than cultures pre-antibiotic if conflict); (3) ADMINISTER BROAD-SPECTRUM ANTIBIOTICS within 1 hour of recognition of sepsis or septic shock — empiric based on suspected source + local resistance patterns + patient factors (this patient = post-op intra-abdominal source — piperacillin-tazobactam 4.5 g IV + vancomycin loading + metronidazole 500 mg IV q8h covers gram-positive + gram-negative + anaerobes including likely Bacteroides + E. coli + Enterococcus + sometimes ESBL Pseudomonas); de-escalate based on cultures + clinical response; (4) BEGIN RAPID ADMINISTRATION of CRYSTALLOID 30 mL/kg IV for hypotension or lactate ≥4 (Lactated Ringer's or balanced solutions preferred over normal saline — less hyperchloremic acidosis; given over ~1 hr; reassess for volume responsiveness using passive leg raise + dynamic measures); (5) APPLY VASOPRESSORS if hypotension persists during or after fluid resuscitation to maintain MAP ≥65 mmHg; NOREPINEPHRINE first-line vasopressor (alpha-1 vasoconstriction + modest beta-1 inotropy; preferred over dopamine — less arrhythmia + better mortality outcomes per CATS trial 2010 + SOAP II); start 0.05 mcg/kg/min titrated to MAP ≥65. EXPANDED MANAGEMENT beyond bundle: ADD VASOPRESSIN 0.04 unit/min FIXED dose (NOT titrated) when norepinephrine reaches 0.25-0.5 mcg/kg/min and MAP still <65 (catecholamine-sparing + vasodilatory shock physiology + improves end-organ perfusion); ADD EPINEPHRINE 0.05-0.5 mcg/kg/min for refractory shock with low cardiac index (inotropic + chronotropic support); avoid high-dose dopamine due to arrhythmias + mortality concerns + phenylephrine 2nd-3rd-line (pure alpha — no inotropy + reflex bradycardia + may decrease cardiac output). HYDROCORTISONE 200 mg/day IV continuous infusion or 50 mg IV q6h for refractory shock not responsive to norepinephrine + vasopressin within ~6 hr (per ADRENAL trial 2018 + APROCCHSS trial 2018 — modest mortality benefit + faster shock reversal); fludrocortisone 50 mcg PO daily added in some protocols. EARLY SOURCE CONTROL critical for outcomes — radiologic + surgical + interventional; for this patient = CT abdomen/pelvis + emergent return to OR for anastomotic leak management. ONGOING CARE: hourly vital signs + UOP + lactate q4-6h + ABG + organ function trending + blood + urine + sputum cultures + de-escalation of antibiotics based on cultures + clinical response (typically 7-10 days for severe sepsis); ARDS surveillance + DVT prophylaxis + stress ulcer prophylaxis + glycemic control 140-180 mg/dL + nutrition + skin + sedation if intubated + family communication.
labs.r: SURVIVING SEPSIS CAMPAIGN 1-HOUR BUNDLE = lactate + cultures + antibiotics within 1 hr + 30 mL/kg crystalloid + vasopressors if needed to MAP ≥65. NOREPINEPHRINE first-line; vasopressin + epinephrine + hydrocortisone for refractory shock. SOURCE CONTROL essential. Time-sensitive = each hour delay associated with increased mortality. RN role: recognize + activate + execute bundle elements + coordinate + monitor + escalate.

labs.v: VASOPRESSOR pharmacology for septic shock: (1) NOREPINEPHRINE (Levophed) — predominantly alpha-1 vasoconstriction (>>beta-1) + modest beta-1 inotropy; FIRST-LINE in septic shock per Surviving Sepsis 2021 + CATS trial 2010 + SOAP II; START 0.05 mcg/kg/min IV via central access (peripheral access acceptable transiently with smaller IV's + reassessed; risk of extravasation = tissue necrosis = phentolamine local treatment) titrated to MAP ≥65; can titrate q5 min during acute resuscitation; typical max ~3 mcg/kg/min before adding agents; preferred over dopamine for arrhythmia + mortality; risks = bradycardia at high doses + peripheral ischemia + arrhythmia + reflex; (2) VASOPRESSIN — antidiuretic hormone analog; FIXED dose 0.04 unit/min (NOT titrated) — adding to norepinephrine when reaching 0.25-0.5 mcg/kg/min and MAP still <65 (catecholamine-sparing + vasopressin deficiency in vasodilatory shock); also for higher MAP target in select patients (75-85) per VANISH/SAVE trials; risk = peripheral + mesenteric + coronary vasoconstriction at higher doses + hyponatremia + decreased cardiac index in some patients; (3) EPINEPHRINE — alpha + beta-1 + beta-2; alpha at higher doses; beta at lower; 0.05-0.5 mcg/kg/min for refractory shock with low cardiac index (inotropic + chronotropic + vasoconstrictive); also code blue arrest 1 mg IV every 3-5 min during ACLS; anaphylaxis 0.3-0.5 mg IM thigh; (4) DOPAMINE — alpha + beta-1 + dopaminergic at variable doses (low 1-3 mcg/kg/min = renal/mesenteric vasodilation — controversial benefit; moderate 5-10 = beta-1 inotropy; high 10-20 = alpha vasoconstriction + arrhythmia + mortality concerns); generally AVOIDED first-line in septic shock per current evidence; reserved for bradycardia + alternative to atropine; (5) PHENYLEPHRINE — pure alpha-1; bolus 100-200 mcg IV or infusion 0.5-2 mcg/kg/min; reflex bradycardia + decreased cardiac output + 2nd-3rd line; useful in selected scenarios (post-cardiac surgery + tachyarrhythmia + intubation hypotension transient); (6) DOBUTAMINE — beta-1 + modest beta-2 inotrope + chronotrope; 2.5-15 mcg/kg/min for cardiogenic shock or septic shock with low cardiac index; vasodilation lowers SVR + can worsen MAP; combined with norepinephrine if needed; (7) MILRINONE — phosphodiesterase-3 inhibitor + inotrope + vasodilator; longer half-life + renal clearance + 2nd-line in cardiogenic shock; rarely used in septic shock primary management. CONTINUOUS MONITORING: arterial line for accurate BP; central line for vasopressor administration + central venous pressure if utility; cardiac monitoring (arrhythmias common with vasopressors); UOP hourly; lactate trending; tissue perfusion assessment (skin + mental status + capillary refill); MAP target ≥65 typical; higher MAP 80-85 in selected patients with chronic HTN or evidence of organ dysfunction. EXTRAVASATION management: STOP infusion + aspirate from IV + leave catheter in place + administer PHENTOLAMINE 5-10 mg in 10-15 mL saline subcutaneously around extravasation site (alpha-blocker reverses vasoconstriction + prevents ischemia + necrosis); warm compress + extremity elevation; consult plastic surgery if severe.
labs.r: VASOPRESSOR ESCALATION = norepinephrine first-line → vasopressin 0.04 fixed at norepi 0.25-0.5 → epinephrine for refractory shock or low CI → hydrocortisone for refractory shock after norepi + vasopressin. Central access ideal. Arterial line. UOP hourly. MAP target ≥65. RN role: titrate to MAP + monitor for adverse effects + recognize extravasation + escalate.

labs.n: SOURCE CONTROL — emergent imaging + surgical intervention
labs.v: SOURCE CONTROL is CRITICAL in septic shock — antimicrobial therapy + resuscitation are bridges to definitive source control. TIMING per Surviving Sepsis = as soon as feasible after hemodynamic stabilization (within 6-12 hr ideal for most septic shock with identifiable source); some sources require IMMEDIATE intervention (necrotizing soft tissue infection, gas gangrene, septic arthritis, perforated viscus). EMERGENT IMAGING: (1) CT ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST = gold standard for intra-abdominal source identification — anastomotic leak, perforation, abscess, ischemic bowel, obstructed bowel, pancreatitis, cholangitis; contrast risk = AKI in already volume-depleted shock patient + balance with diagnostic urgency; (2) CT CHEST for pneumonia/empyema; (3) US for pleural/abdominal fluid + biliary; (4) MRI not typically emergent imaging in shock; (5) cardiac echo for septic cardiomyopathy + endocarditis evaluation; (6) imaging not delayed for hemodynamic instability if necessary for source identification + treatment. ANASTOMOTIC LEAK identification = extra-luminal contrast + free air + abscess collection + peritonitis; management depends on patient stability + leak severity + extent of contamination + days post-op + surgeon judgment; OPTIONS: (1) DIVERTING ILEOSTOMY/COLOSTOMY = standard for moderate-severe leaks with peritonitis — diverts fecal stream + allows leak to heal + protects further injury + planned reversal 3-6 months later if recovery good; (2) ANASTOMOSIS TAKEDOWN with end colostomy + Hartmann's procedure = severe peritonitis + unstable patient + planned reversal months later; (3) PRIMARY REPAIR + drainage = early small leak in stable patient (less common); (4) PERCUTANEOUS DRAINAGE for contained abscess without significant systemic effects + selected stable patients; (5) ANTIBIOTICS alone for very small contained leaks in selected stable patients (rare). PERIOPERATIVE care: optimize hemodynamics + electrolytes + coagulation + glucose before OR; intubation for airway protection during transport + procedure; FFP if INR >1.5 + planned procedure (this patient INR pending; preparation); blood product availability; NG decompression; antibiotic continuation; family + surgical consent; OR team activation. POST-OP ICU: hemodynamic monitoring + vasopressor titration + ventilation + nutrition + drains output + wound care + infection surveillance + antibiotic continuation + de-escalation per culture + clinical response + nutrition (parenteral + transition to enteral as tolerated) + glycemic control + DVT prophylaxis + stress ulcer prophylaxis + skin + early mobilization when stable + multidisciplinary care + family communication.
labs.r: SOURCE CONTROL essential in septic shock — antimicrobials + resuscitation are bridges. EMERGENT IMAGING (CT) identifies source. SURGICAL intervention for anastomotic leak (diverting ileostomy + Hartmann's + primary repair + percutaneous drainage based on patient + leak severity + stability). PERIOPERATIVE optimization + post-op ICU care. RN role: facilitate transport + advocate for early imaging + perioperative coordination.

labs.v: DELEGATION in nursing practice per ANA principles + state nurse practice acts: 5 RIGHTS of DELEGATION (Right Task + Right Person + Right Circumstance + Right Communication + Right Supervision); FOUNDATION = delegation requires assessment + judgment + cannot delegate the nursing process steps requiring critical thinking (assessment, planning, evaluation, complex teaching); CAN delegate stable patient tasks within receiving person's scope. RN SCOPE = comprehensive assessment + analysis + diagnosis + planning + intervention + evaluation + teaching + advocacy + IV medications + blood + chemotherapy + complex wounds + initial assessments + critical care + medications requiring complex monitoring/titration + IV push medications + central line access + arterial line + emergent care. LPN/LVN SCOPE (varies by state — California Vocational Nurse Practice Act allows): stable medication administration (oral + IM + SQ + IV per CA waiver completion; not initial IV push of high-risk meds; not titrating vasopressors) + dressings + vital signs assessment + reinforce education already provided + Foley + NG + suctioning + tracheostomy care + glucose monitoring + observation; LPN/LVN does NOT do initial nursing assessment + complex assessment + IV push of vasoactive/blood/insulin/chemo + central lines + arterial lines + initial blood transfusion verification + complex titration + initial patient teaching (can reinforce after RN does initial). UAP/CNA SCOPE = vital signs in STABLE patients + ADLs + ambulation + positioning + skin care basic + I/O + meal trays + supply transport + bedmaking + reinforce safety; CANNOT do assessment + medication administration + complex care + glucose monitoring + diabetic patient education + sterile procedures. RIGHT TASK examples for septic shock RRT response — RN tasks: IV vasopressor titration + central line care + arterial line monitoring + ABG interpretation + medication administration + family communication + handoff to ICU; LPN/LVN tasks: vital signs in stable patients on floor + medication administration after RN assessment + dressing changes + supportive care; CNA tasks: VS in stable patients + ambulating roommate + supplies + transport. RIGHT PERSON = matches task to scope + competency + experience + assignment; can refuse if outside scope or unsafe; RIGHT CIRCUMSTANCE = appropriate stability + acuity for delegated task; complex/unstable = RN only; RIGHT COMMUNICATION = clear task + outcome expectations + timeframe + when to report back; RIGHT SUPERVISION = available + responsive RN supervision + feedback + review; documentation. CHAIN OF COMMAND for delegation concerns or refusals: charge RN → unit manager → CNO/Chief Nursing Officer → Medical Director; document concerns. SAFE STAFFING ratios per California Title 22 = ICU 1:2, telemetry 1:4, med-surg 1:5, OR depends, ED varies. UNSAFE DELEGATION = RN delegates outside person's scope (e.g., asking CNA to monitor titrating vasopressors), delegates without supervision (no available RN), delegates complex/unstable patient task to LPN/CNA, fails to verify person's competency.
labs.r: DELEGATION is RN-centered + critical to safe care + 5 RIGHTS (Task + Person + Circumstance + Communication + Supervision); RN/LPN/CNA scope per state nurse practice act + ANA principles; CAN delegate stable tasks + CANNOT delegate nursing process critical thinking; chain of command for concerns. RN role: appropriate delegation + supervision + advocacy + safety + documentation + competency verification.

labs.n: FAMILY communication + informed consent for emergent surgery
labs.v: FAMILY-CENTERED communication during acute decompensation + emergent surgery: (1) IMMEDIATE notification by family liaison or CNS to family of acute change + RRT activation + likely transfer to ICU; (2) HONEST + COMPASSIONATE updates with severity acknowledged + plan + emotional support; (3) FAMILY MEETING when stable + patient + spouse + family present (wife arrived 1530; adult daughter arrived 1650; adult son arriving en route); intensivist + surgeon + bedside RN present; explanation of: anastomotic leak diagnosis + need for emergent return to OR + procedure description (diverting ileostomy + Hartmann's vs primary repair options + drain placement + washout) + risks (mortality 15-25% in post-op anastomotic leak with septic shock + complications + potential prolonged ICU stay + recovery + possible permanent ileostomy if reversal not feasible) + benefits (source control essential to survival + appropriate management); (4) INFORMED CONSENT — patient is decisional currently (alert + oriented + understanding despite altered mental status from sepsis recovering with treatment); CAN consent personally before sedation/intubation; OR spouse as healthcare proxy if patient incapacitated; document discussion + risks + benefits + alternatives + patient/proxy understanding + questions + consent + signature; for emergent surgery if patient incapacitated + spouse not available — institutional + state law provisions for emergency exception (substituted consent or emergency exception when no time + clear benefit); HIPAA permits disclosure to family per patient consent + need-to-know basis; (5) ANXIETY + GRIEF support from chaplain + social worker; CULTURAL + RELIGIOUS considerations honored; family rituals supported; (6) DOCUMENTATION of communications + decision-making process + consent; (7) CONTINUED communication during surgery + in ICU + ongoing recovery + multidisciplinary engagement + family meetings as needed. THIS PATIENT: wife at bedside 1530 (arrived from home 30 min after notification); adult daughter (RN at outside hospital — has medical literacy + asking specific clinical questions) arrived 1650; adult son arriving en route from work 1730; family supportive + appropriate + asking informed questions; patient has decisional capacity for surgery consent — informed consent obtained directly with patient at 1700 with wife present; spouse co-signs as healthcare proxy; informed consent for OR + intubation + lines + transfusion + risks discussed + understood + agreed; chaplain offered + accepted (family identifies as Christian + church community present); social worker + family liaison continued throughout.
labs.r: FAMILY-CENTERED communication during acute decompensation = immediate notification + honest updates + family meeting + informed consent + emotional/spiritual support + cultural sensitivity + documentation + continued engagement. Patient autonomy + capacity + healthcare proxy + emergency exception when applicable. RN role: facilitate + advocate + emotional support + ensure understanding + communication coordination.

labs.n: POST-OPERATIVE COMPLICATIONS + ICU care after source control
labs.v: POST-OPERATIVE care for patient with sepsis + emergent surgery: (1) IMMEDIATE post-op continuation of sepsis management bundle elements + vasopressor titration + ventilator if intubated + monitoring; (2) HEMODYNAMIC OPTIMIZATION = MAP ≥65 with vasopressors (norepi + vasopressin + epi if needed) + lactate trending q4-6h + UOP hourly + cardiac monitoring + arterial line + central line; (3) VENTILATION = if intubated, lung-protective Vt 6-8 mL/kg IBW + PEEP 5-10 + FiO2 to SpO2 94-98% + lung-protective settings + serial ABGs + sedation per RASS -3 to 0 + spontaneous awakening + breathing trials daily + early extubation when ready + post-extubation supplemental O2 + non-invasive support if needed; (4) ANTIBIOTICS = continue broad-spectrum + de-escalate based on cultures (typically 7-10 days for complicated intra-abdominal sepsis per IDSA + Surviving Sepsis); switch IV to PO when appropriate; ID consult; (5) SOURCE control follow-up = drain output monitoring + character + amount + color; surgical wound care; ileostomy care + education + supplies + appliance fitting; potential percutaneous drainage of additional collections; ID + surgery rounds; (6) METABOLIC + GLYCEMIC = control 140-180 mg/dL with insulin drip per protocol; electrolytes + replace; nutrition (parenteral nutrition initiated within 24-48 hr post-op + transition to enteral via NG/Dobhoff/J-tube as bowel function returns + oral diet later); thiamine + multivitamins; (7) RENAL = AKI prevention + management; avoid nephrotoxins + contrast unless essential + CRRT if AKI severe + fluid balance; (8) RESPIRATORY = lung-protective vent + DVT prophylaxis + stress ulcer prophylaxis; (9) NEUROLOGIC = sedation/analgesia titration + delirium prevention (PADIS bundle — pain + agitation/sedation + delirium + immobility + sleep); CAM-ICU q shift; (10) HEMATOLOGIC = transfusion as needed (target Hgb >7-8); coagulopathy management; (11) ENDOCRINE = continue hydrocortisone for refractory shock + taper as shock reverses; thyroid if hypothermic + euthyroid sick syndrome surveillance; (12) GASTROINTESTINAL = NPO initially → enteral nutrition early as bowel returns + ileostomy output monitoring; (13) MOBILITY = early mobility when hemodynamically stable + PT/OT involvement; (14) SKIN = pressure injury prevention + Braden + skin care + offloading; (15) INFECTION = surveillance + bundle adherence (HAI prevention bundle); (16) FAMILY = ongoing communication + meetings + emotional support + spiritual care; (17) MULTIDISCIPLINARY rounds + handoffs + clear communication. PROGNOSIS for post-op anastomotic leak with septic shock = 15-25% mortality; surviving patients typically have prolonged ICU + hospital + recovery; permanent ileostomy in ~30% (others undergo reversal at 3-6 months). LONG-TERM care + outpatient follow-up + ileostomy education + dietary modifications + colorectal surgery follow-up + ID + PCP + nutrition + psychological support.
labs.r: POST-OP ICU care after source control = continued sepsis management + hemodynamic optimization + ventilation + antibiotics + nutrition + delirium prevention + DVT/stress ulcer prophylaxis + ileostomy care + family + multidisciplinary. Mortality 15-25% in post-op anastomotic leak with septic shock. Prolonged ICU + hospital + recovery. RN role: continuous bedside care + multidisciplinary coordination + family + monitoring + escalation.

steps.title: Recognize Cues
steps.sub: What matters at this RRT activation?
steps.inst: Select ALL findings that are clinically + team-significant cues requiring nursing attention.

opts.text: PROGRESSIVE deterioration from POD 2 0700 (HR 94 + T 37.8 — early SIRS) → 1200 (HR 108 + T 38.2 + decreased UOP + abdominal pain — SIRS criteria + concerning) → 1430 (HR 128 + BP 84/52 + RR 28 + SpO2 92% + T 38.8 + CONFUSION + lactate 4.2 + oliguria + peritoneal signs — SEPTIC SHOCK with end-organ dysfunction) requires immediate RRT activation + Surviving Sepsis 1-hr bundle + source identification + surgical consultation
opts.rat: PRIORITY CUE — progressive deterioration recognition. EARLY recognition + escalation is critical for sepsis outcomes. Pattern of progression from early SIRS → established sepsis → septic shock requires immediate action. RN role: trend recognition + escalation + activation.

opts.text: RRT ACTIVATION criteria objectively met: NEWS2 score ≥7 (HR 3 + RR 3 + SpO2 2 + temp 1 + consciousness 4 = 13); SIRS all 4 criteria + qSOFA ≥2 (SBP <100 + RR ≥22 + altered mental status); hemodynamic instability + altered mental status + RN concern — all valid criteria; primary RN STAYS with patient + initiates immediate stabilization while awaiting team (supplemental O2 + IV bolus + repeated VS + family notification preparation + chart preparation)

opts.text: Allergies: PCN (rash + hives 20 years ago — never reproduced + tolerated cephalosporins per chart cross-reactivity risk ~2% with no prior reaction); document and proceed; piperacillin-tazobactam acceptable with caution per institutional protocol since carbapenem-class antibiotics are alternatives + cross-reactivity with piperacillin minimal
opts.rat: Allergy history documentation. PCN rash documented + non-anaphylactic. Cross-reactivity with cephalosporins + piperacillin minimal in non-anaphylactic PCN allergy. Document and proceed. Not the priority cue but important for safety.

opts.text: DELAY initiating broad-spectrum antibiotics until blood culture results are available (typically 24-48 hr) — empirical treatment without confirmation could expose patient to unnecessary side effects + contribute to antibiotic resistance
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG + DANGEROUS approach. SURVIVING SEPSIS CAMPAIGN 1-HOUR BUNDLE requires BROAD-SPECTRUM ANTIBIOTICS WITHIN 1 HOUR of recognition; each hour delay associated with increased mortality (~7% per hour). Cultures drawn BEFORE antibiotics WHEN POSSIBLE without delaying antibiotic >45 min. Empirical broad-spectrum coverage essential + de-escalate based on cultures + clinical response. Standard evidence-based practice + saves lives.

opts.text: DELEGATE the vasopressor titration to the LPN/LVN because they are competent with medication administration + freeing up the RN for documentation + family communication
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach + outside LPN/LVN scope of practice. VASOPRESSOR titration requires RN-level skills (assessment, judgment, hemodynamic interpretation, dose adjustment, side effect recognition, escalation) + is RN-only task per scope; LPN/LVN cannot independently titrate vasoactive medications. RN must titrate + supervise + delegate appropriately. Inappropriate delegation = patient safety risk + nursing scope violation.

steps.title: Analyze Cues
steps.sub: What sepsis + team activation + delegation principles apply?
steps.inst: Select ALL accurate linkages between cues and underlying principles.

opts.text: SEPSIS RECOGNITION criteria: SIRS (T >38 or <36 + HR >90 + RR >20 or PaCO2 <32 + WBC >12k or <4k or >10% bands — 2+ criteria); qSOFA (SBP <100 + RR ≥22 + altered mental status — 2+ criteria predicts mortality); SEPSIS = SIRS + suspected/confirmed infection; SEVERE SEPSIS = sepsis + organ dysfunction; SEPTIC SHOCK = sepsis + persistent hypotension requiring vasopressors to MAP ≥65 + lactate >2 despite adequate fluid resuscitation; NEWS2 scoring composite for early warning
opts.rat: CORRECT — sepsis recognition framework. Multiple criteria + scoring systems + progression definitions. Foundation for early recognition + RRT activation + bundle initiation.

opts.text: RAPID RESPONSE TEAM (RRT) = structured early-warning + rapid intervention system to reduce unanticipated ICU transfers + cardiac arrests + mortality; activation criteria include NEWS2 ≥7 + SIRS + qSOFA ≥2 + significant vital sign change + altered mental status + decreased UOP + chest pain + new dysrhythmia + stroke symptoms + seizure + RN concern; team composition + roles + closed-loop communication + SBAR + documentation + post-event debriefing
opts.rat: CORRECT — RRT framework. Structured team approach. Evidence-based. Foundation for managing acute deterioration.

opts.text: SURVIVING SEPSIS CAMPAIGN 1-HOUR BUNDLE (2021): (1) lactate; (2) blood cultures before antibiotics; (3) broad-spectrum antibiotics within 1 hr; (4) crystalloid 30 mL/kg if hypotension or lactate ≥4; (5) vasopressors if hypotension persists to MAP ≥65; NOREPINEPHRINE first-line; VASOPRESSIN 0.04 fixed at norepi 0.25-0.5; HYDROCORTISONE refractory after norepi + vasopressin; EPINEPHRINE for low CI refractory
opts.rat: CORRECT — bundle elements + time-sensitive. Evidence-based mortality benefit. Foundation for management.

opts.text: VASOPRESSOR PHARMACOLOGY: NOREPINEPHRINE first-line alpha-1 + modest beta-1 (less arrhythmia + better mortality vs dopamine); VASOPRESSIN ANTIDIURETIC HORMONE analog fixed 0.04 (catecholamine-sparing + vasodilatory shock physiology); EPINEPHRINE refractory shock with low CI; DOPAMINE avoided first-line (arrhythmia + mortality); PHENYLEPHRINE 2nd-3rd line pure alpha; HYDROCORTISONE for refractory; extravasation = phentolamine antidote
opts.rat: CORRECT — vasopressor pharmacology framework. Mechanism + escalation + monitoring + alternatives + extravasation. Foundation for hemodynamic management.

opts.text: SOURCE CONTROL is CRITICAL in septic shock — antimicrobials + resuscitation are bridges to definitive source control. Imaging (CT for intra-abdominal + chest for pulmonary + US for biliary + echo for endocarditis) + surgical/interventional intervention; ANASTOMOTIC LEAK = post-op surgical complication 3-15% incidence requiring emergent return to OR for washout + diverting ileostomy + drain + sometimes takedown of anastomosis (Hartmann's)
opts.rat: CORRECT — source control framework. Imaging + intervention. Anastomotic leak management. Time-sensitive. Foundation for survival.

opts.text: DELEGATION 5 RIGHTS (Right Task + Right Person + Right Circumstance + Right Communication + Right Supervision); RN scope = comprehensive assessment + analysis + diagnosis + planning + intervention + evaluation + teaching + advocacy + IV medications + complex titration + central + arterial lines; LPN/LVN = stable medication + dressings + VS + reinforce education (not initial assessment or complex critical care); CNA/UAP = VS stable patients + ADLs + ambulation + supplies (not assessment or medication administration); CHAIN OF COMMAND for concerns
opts.rat: CORRECT — delegation framework + scope of practice + 5 Rights + chain of command. RN central role. Appropriate task assignment + supervision. Foundation for safe care.

opts.text: FAMILY-CENTERED COMMUNICATION + INFORMED CONSENT: immediate notification + honest updates + family meeting + informed consent + emotional + spiritual support + cultural sensitivity + documentation + continued engagement; patient autonomy + capacity + healthcare proxy + emergency exception when applicable; HIPAA + appropriate information sharing per consent + need-to-know basis
opts.rat: CORRECT — family-centered framework. Patient autonomy + family engagement + multidisciplinary + emotional support + ethics. Foundation for ongoing care + decision-making.

opts.text: DELAY antibiotic administration until blood culture results are available — empirical treatment is irresponsible + contributes to antibiotic resistance + side effects
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. SURVIVING SEPSIS CAMPAIGN requires BROAD-SPECTRUM ANTIBIOTICS WITHIN 1 HOUR. Each hour delay associated with increased mortality. Cultures BEFORE antibiotics when possible without delaying antibiotic >45 min. Empirical broad-spectrum + de-escalate per cultures + clinical response = standard evidence-based practice + saves lives.

opts.text: DELEGATE the IV vasopressor titration + central line care to LPN/LVN to free up the RN for family communication + documentation
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach + outside LPN/LVN scope of practice. Vasopressor titration + central line care + complex critical care = RN-only tasks per nurse practice act. LPN/LVN scope is stable medication administration + dressings + VS + reinforced education. Delegating complex/unstable patient tasks = patient safety risk + scope violation.

opts.text: Initiate DOPAMINE 5 mcg/kg/min as first-line vasopressor for this patient + add vasopressin only at norepinephrine 1.0 mcg/kg/min + skip hydrocortisone since no clear evidence + use albumin instead of LR for fluid resuscitation
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach in multiple aspects. Dopamine NOT first-line per current Surviving Sepsis 2021 (arrhythmia + mortality concerns); NOREPINEPHRINE first-line. VASOPRESSIN 0.04 fixed dose added at norepi 0.25-0.5 (NOT 1.0). HYDROCORTISONE has modest mortality benefit + faster shock reversal per ADRENAL + APROCCHSS — recommended for refractory shock not responsive to norepi + vasopressin within ~6 hr. LR or balanced solutions preferred over normal saline (less hyperchloremic acidosis) — albumin can be used but more expensive + less evidence for routine first-line + not standard. Standard practice: NOREPI → VASOPRESSIN 0.04 → EPI → HYDROCORTISONE.

steps.title: Prioritize Hypotheses
steps.sub: Rank nursing concerns at this RRT activation
steps.inst: Rank these nursing concerns from #1 (HIGHEST priority) to #5 (LOWEST).

opts.text: Septic Shock with Hemodynamic Instability r/t anastomotic leak; Surviving Sepsis 1-hr bundle (lactate + cultures + broad-spectrum antibiotics within 1 hr + crystalloid 30 mL/kg + vasopressors to MAP ≥65); NOREPINEPHRINE first-line; VASOPRESSIN 0.04 fixed + HYDROCORTISONE for refractory shock; central + arterial access; continuous monitoring + escalation
opts.rat: #1 — HIGHEST. Septic shock is the immediate life threat. Time-sensitive bundle. Each hour delay associated with mortality. RN role: rapid recognition + bundle execution + hemodynamic management + escalation.

opts.text: Source Control + Emergent Surgery r/t anastomotic leak confirmed on CT (POD 2 sigmoid colectomy site with collection + free air + peritonitis); emergent return to OR for washout + diverting ileostomy + drain + possible Hartmann's; pre-OR optimization + intubation in OR; post-op ICU care + continuation
opts.rat: #2 — HIGH. Source control essential to survival in septic shock. Antibiotics + resuscitation are bridges. Emergent surgical intervention. Without source control, sepsis recovery limited.

opts.text: RRT Team Leadership + Closed-Loop Communication r/t structured team activation + role assignments + SBAR handoff + multidisciplinary coordination + post-event debriefing; CHAIN OF COMMAND (primary RN → charge RN → RRT → intensivist → surgical attending); DELEGATION 5 RIGHTS (Right Task + Person + Circumstance + Communication + Supervision)
opts.rat: #3 — HIGH. Team coordination + delegation + chain of command. Structured response improves outcomes. Foundation for safe + effective care. RN central role.

opts.text: Family-Centered Communication + Informed Consent r/t wife (healthcare proxy) + adult daughter (RN at outside hospital with medical literacy) + adult son arriving; family meeting with intensivist + surgeon + bedside RN; informed consent for emergent surgery + risks + benefits + alternatives + emotional support + chaplain + social worker; cultural + spiritual considerations
opts.rat: #4 — Significant + ongoing. Family-centered care + informed consent + emotional support throughout. Patient autonomy + family engagement. Multidisciplinary engagement. Foundation for ongoing care.

opts.text: ICU Bundle + Post-Op Care r/t continued post-OR care with hemodynamic monitoring + ventilator management + antibiotics + nutrition + delirium prevention + DVT/stress ulcer prophylaxis + ileostomy care + early mobility + outpatient follow-up + multidisciplinary engagement; long-term recovery + potential ileostomy reversal at 3-6 months
opts.rat: #5 — Ongoing + foundational. Comprehensive ICU + post-op care + recovery. Foundation for long-term outcome. Mortality 15-25% in post-op anastomotic leak septic shock — most patients survive with comprehensive care.

steps.title: Generate Solutions
steps.inst: For EACH intervention, classify as INDICATED or NOT INDICATED for this patient.

opts.text: RAPID RESPONSE TEAM ACTIVATION + team roles (intensivist + critical care RN + RT + pharmacy + primary RN + charge RN + UAP + house officer) + closed-loop communication + SBAR + post-event debriefing within 30 min + formal review 24-48 hr; primary RN STAYS with patient + initial stabilization while awaiting team
opts.rat: Standard evidence-based RRT framework. Reduces mortality + unanticipated transfers + cardiac arrests. RN empowered to activate. Foundation for managing acute deterioration.

opts.text: SURVIVING SEPSIS CAMPAIGN 1-HOUR BUNDLE: lactate + cultures + broad-spectrum antibiotics (piperacillin-tazobactam + vancomycin + metronidazole for intra-abdominal source) within 1 hr + crystalloid 30 mL/kg + NOREPINEPHRINE titrated to MAP ≥65; vasopressin 0.04 fixed at norepi 0.25-0.5; hydrocortisone for refractory shock; central + arterial access
opts.rat: Evidence-based 1-hr bundle per 2021 Surviving Sepsis Campaign. Time-sensitive intervention. Mortality benefit. Foundation for sepsis management.

opts.text: EMERGENT IMAGING (CT abdomen/pelvis with IV contrast) for source identification + general surgery consultation + emergent return to OR for washout + diverting ileostomy + drain + possible Hartmann's procedure; pre-OR optimization + intubation; post-op ICU continuation
opts.rat: Source control essential in septic shock. Emergent imaging + surgery. Standard management for anastomotic leak with peritonitis.

opts.text: DELEGATION per 5 RIGHTS: RN tasks (vasopressor titration + central line + arterial line + complex assessment + medication administration + family communication + handoff) vs LPN/LVN tasks (stable medication + dressings + VS in stable patients) vs CNA/UAP tasks (VS stable patients + ADLs + supplies + transport); CHAIN OF COMMAND for delegation concerns
opts.rat: Standard delegation framework. Scope of practice + 5 Rights + supervision + chain of command. RN central role in complex critical care. Foundation for safe care.

opts.text: FAMILY-CENTERED communication with wife (healthcare proxy) + adult daughter (RN at outside hospital) + adult son; family meeting with intensivist + surgeon + bedside RN; INFORMED CONSENT for emergent surgery + risks + benefits + alternatives + understanding + chaplain + social worker + emotional + spiritual support
opts.rat: Family-centered care + informed consent. Patient autonomy + family engagement. Multidisciplinary engagement. Foundation for ongoing care.

opts.text: DELAY broad-spectrum antibiotics until blood culture results are available (24-48 hr) to avoid unnecessary side effects + antibiotic resistance
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG + DANGEROUS. Surviving Sepsis Campaign requires broad-spectrum antibiotics WITHIN 1 HOUR. Each hour delay associated with mortality (~7% per hour). Cultures BEFORE antibiotics when possible without delaying antibiotic >45 min. Empirical coverage + de-escalate per cultures = standard evidence-based + saves lives.

opts.text: DELEGATE vasopressor titration + central line management + complex critical care to LPN/LVN to free up RN for documentation + family communication
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. Vasopressor titration + central line + complex critical care = RN-only tasks per nurse practice act. LPN/LVN scope is stable medication + dressings + VS in stable patients. Inappropriate delegation = patient safety risk + scope violation. RN must perform + supervise + delegate appropriately.

opts.text: Initiate DOPAMINE 5 mcg/kg/min as first-line vasopressor in this patient because of historical use + familiarity; add other agents only if dopamine fails
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach. NOREPINEPHRINE first-line per current Surviving Sepsis 2021 + CATS trial + SOAP II — better mortality + fewer arrhythmias than dopamine. Dopamine reserved for bradycardia + alternative to atropine. Standard evidence-based: NOREPI → VASOPRESSIN 0.04 → EPI → HYDROCORTISONE.

opts.text: CT abdomen/pelvis with IV contrast for source identification is contraindicated in this hemodynamically unstable patient — wait for hemodynamic stability + use ultrasound instead
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG. CT abdomen/pelvis is the gold standard for intra-abdominal source identification + IS PERFORMED in hemodynamically unstable patients when diagnostic urgency outweighs contrast nephrotoxicity risk — balance with adequate hydration + vasopressor support during transport + monitoring during scan. Ultrasound less sensitive for anastomotic leak. Source control is essential — diagnostic delay = mortality. Standard practice.

opts.text: HYDROCORTISONE 200 mg/day IV continuous infusion or 50 mg IV q6h for refractory shock not responsive to norepinephrine + vasopressin within ~6 hr per ADRENAL + APROCCHSS trials — modest mortality benefit + faster shock reversal
opts.rat: Standard adjunct for refractory septic shock per Surviving Sepsis 2021 + ADRENAL + APROCCHSS evidence. Modest mortality benefit + faster shock reversal. RN role: administer + monitor + escalate.

opts.text: Apply 4-point restraints to prevent patient from removing arterial line + central line + IV lines during septic encephalopathy
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG approach + violates restraint-free care standards. Restraints in ICU should be LEAST RESTRICTIVE + only for true safety emergencies + time-limited + documented + alternatives tried + CMS regulated. ALTERNATIVES = 1:1 sitter for confused patient + environmental safety + clear communication + family + appropriate sedation if needed + de-escalation + addressing source of agitation (pain + fluid + altered mental status from sepsis improves with treatment). Standard institutional restraint-free care + dignity preservation.

opts.text: Initiate ICU bundle care: HOB ≥30°, oral chlorhexidine q12h + oral care q4h, CHG bath daily + central line + Foley necessity assessment daily, sedation interruption + SAT + SBT daily, early mobility when stable, PT/OT bedside, family communication, multidisciplinary rounds daily, DVT prophylaxis (mechanical SCDs initially + transition to heparin when bleeding risk low), stress ulcer prophylaxis (pantoprazole 40 mg IV daily)
opts.rat: Standard ICU bundle care + HAI prevention + delirium prevention + DVT/stress ulcer prophylaxis + multidisciplinary rounds. Foundation for safe + effective ICU care + reduces complications.

steps.title: Take Action
steps.sub: FIRST action sequence during RRT activation
steps.inst: Rank the order in which the RN should implement these actions. #1 = FIRST.

opts.text: RECOGNIZE acute deterioration + ACTIVATE RAPID RESPONSE TEAM via institutional phone/PA; primary RN STAYS with patient + initiates immediate stabilization while awaiting team — supplemental O2 + IV bolus + repeated VS + family notification preparation + chart preparation; CHARGE RN notified + emergency cart bedside; safety + communication
opts.rat: #1 — FIRST. Immediate recognition + activation + stabilization. Each minute counts in sepsis. RN role: recognize + activate + initial stabilization + communication.

opts.text: EXECUTE Surviving Sepsis 1-HOUR BUNDLE: (1) lactate level + recheck q4-6h; (2) blood cultures × 2 + urine + sputum cultures before antibiotics (without delaying antibiotic >45 min); (3) broad-spectrum antibiotics within 1 hr (piperacillin-tazobactam + vancomycin + metronidazole for intra-abdominal source); (4) IV crystalloid 30 mL/kg LR over 1 hr; (5) NOREPINEPHRINE 0.05 mcg/kg/min titrated to MAP ≥65; CENTRAL + arterial access; FOLEY
opts.rat: #2 — Second. Time-sensitive bundle elements. Each element associated with mortality benefit. RN role: bundle execution + medication administration + access + monitoring.

opts.text: SOURCE CONTROL = emergent CT abdomen/pelvis with IV contrast for source identification → ANASTOMOTIC LEAK confirmed → general surgery consultation urgent → emergent return to OR for washout + diverting ileostomy + drain + possible Hartmann's; OR holding + anesthesia + nursing OR team activated; pre-OR hemodynamic optimization + intubation in OR; perioperative coordination
opts.rat: #3 — Third. Source control essential. Imaging + surgical intervention. Time-sensitive for survival. Multidisciplinary coordination.

opts.text: VASOPRESSOR ESCALATION + HEMODYNAMIC management: NOREPINEPHRINE titrated to MAP ≥65; VASOPRESSIN 0.04 unit/min FIXED added at norepi 0.25-0.5 mcg/kg/min; HYDROCORTISONE 50 mg IV q6h for refractory shock not responsive to norepi + vasopressin within ~6 hr; central + arterial access; lactate trending; UOP hourly; tissue perfusion + mental status
opts.rat: #4 — Fourth. Vasopressor escalation + hemodynamic management. Catecholamine-sparing + adjuvant therapy. RN role: titration + monitoring + escalation.

opts.text: FAMILY COMMUNICATION + INFORMED CONSENT + MULTIDISCIPLINARY: family liaison/CNS notifies family of acute change + RRT + transfer to ICU + arrival; family meeting with intensivist + surgeon + bedside RN; informed consent for emergent surgery + risks + benefits + alternatives + emotional support + chaplain + social worker + cultural + spiritual support; continued communication during surgery + ICU
opts.rat: #5 — Ongoing + foundational. Family-centered care + informed consent. Patient autonomy + family engagement + multidisciplinary + emotional support. Foundation for ongoing care.

steps.title: Evaluate Outcomes
steps.inst: For each finding, classify the trajectory.

opts.text: POD 2 1700 (3 hr post-RRT): HR 106 (declining tachycardia from 128), BP 100/62 MAP 75 (improved from 84/52), RR 20, SpO2 97% on 4L NC, T 38.4°C; norepinephrine 0.18 + vasopressin 0.04 fixed added 1620 + hydrocortisone 50 mg IV q6h initiated 1645; lactate 2.4 (improving from 4.2); UOP 45 mL/hr (improving from <20); family meeting completed + informed consent obtained + OR pending 1730
opts.rat: Hemodynamic improvement + lactate decreasing + UOP recovering + family engaged + informed consent obtained + surgery preparation. Standard expected response to bundle + vasopressor escalation + family-centered care. RN role: continued bedside care + escalation.

opts.text: POD 2 1900 (post-OR): patient s/p emergent return to OR for sigmoid leak washout + diverting ileostomy + drain placement + Hartmann's NOT done (anastomosis revised — primary anastomosis kept with diverting loop ileostomy); intubated + sedated + transferred back to ICU; norepinephrine 0.20 + vasopressin 0.04 + hydrocortisone continuing; antibiotics continuing; lactate 1.8 (downtrending); blood loss 400 mL; family at bedside + reassured
opts.rat: Successful source control + appropriate surgical management. Standard post-op trajectory + continued ICU care. Family + multidisciplinary engagement. Foundation for recovery.

opts.text: POD 4 morning (2 days post-OR): patient extubated + alert + oriented x3 + minimal vasopressor (norepinephrine 0.05 mcg/kg/min weaning + vasopressin discontinued + hydrocortisone tapering); broad-spectrum antibiotics continued + de-escalation pending cultures; lactate 1.2 (normalized); UOP 80 mL/hr; ileostomy output watery + improving; nutrition initiated + enteral feeding + advancing diet; physical therapy bedside; family present + supportive
opts.rat: Standard expected post-op recovery + weaning vasopressors + extubation + nutrition advancement + multidisciplinary care + family engagement. Successful management of complex sepsis + source control. Foundation for ongoing recovery.

opts.text: POD 7 (5 days post-OR): patient transferred from ICU to step-down unit + 2 days later to regular surgical floor; ileostomy care education + supplies + appliance fitting + dietary modifications + insurance + community resources; outpatient follow-up scheduled; antibiotics completed + cultures grew E. coli + Bacteroides + Enterococcus sensitive to current regimen + de-escalated; PT/OT progressing + mobility improving; nutrition stable + diet advancing; family supportive + adult daughter helping with education
opts.rat: Standard expected post-op recovery trajectory. ICU step-down + floor + ileostomy care + multidisciplinary discharge planning + antibiotic stewardship. Foundation for safe discharge + ongoing recovery.

opts.text: POD 2 1530 alternative scenario — RN incorrectly delegates IV vasopressor titration to LPN/LVN due to time pressure during RRT; LPN/LVN titrates norepinephrine from 0.05 → 1.5 mcg/kg/min within 30 min seeking quick MAP improvement; patient develops profound peripheral vasoconstriction + digital ischemia + arrhythmia; charge RN identifies delegation error + escalates to intensivist + RN assumes titration responsibility + medication safety review + protocol education + just culture
opts.rat: ⚠️ DELEGATION ERROR — LPN/LVN scope does not include IV vasopressor titration. Inappropriate delegation caused patient harm (peripheral vasoconstriction + ischemia + arrhythmia from over-titration). RN scope = vasopressor titration. Standard medication safety review + protocol education + just culture + system improvement. Demonstrates importance of 5 Rights of delegation.

opts.text: POD 3 day 1 post-OR: patient develops acute kidney injury with Cr 2.8 (up from baseline 1.0 + admission 1.4); UOP <30 mL/hr × 4 hr; nephrology consult activated + CRRT (continuous renal replacement therapy) initiated for AKI + fluid balance + uremia management; CRRT preferred over IHD for hemodynamic stability + tolerated by patient; vasopressor support continued; antibiotics renal-dosed + adjusted
opts.rat: Acute kidney injury is COMMON complication of septic shock (~30-50%). CRRT preferred over IHD for hemodynamic stability + fluid balance. Antibiotic renal dosing. Standard complication + appropriate management. RN role: bedside monitoring + escalation + CRRT support.

opts.text: POD 14 (12 days post-OR): patient discharged home with home health for ileostomy care + dressing changes + IV antibiotic completion via PICC line; ileostomy bag + appliance + supplies + nutrition + dietary modifications + colorectal surgery follow-up at 2 weeks + 6 weeks + 3-6 months for ileostomy reversal evaluation; outpatient ID + PCP + nutrition + psychological support if needed; insurance + community resources
opts.rat: Standard discharge trajectory + comprehensive care + outpatient follow-up + community resources. Foundation for ongoing recovery + potential ileostomy reversal at 3-6 months. Multidisciplinary engagement achieved goals. Standard ideal outcome.

opts.text: Post-event RRT debriefing within 30 min after stabilization + formal review at 24-48 hr: team identifies recognition gap (POD 2 0700 early signs of HR 94 + T 37.8 + new abdominal discomfort were under-recognized) + system improvement opportunities + protocol refinement; SBAR + chain of command + delegation appropriate throughout; medication safety reinforced; family communication effective; just culture + system learning
opts.rat: Standard post-event debriefing + just culture + system learning. Recognition gap identified for improvement (early subtle signs deserve attention). RRT response effective. Foundation for continued institutional learning + improvement.

opts.text: 3-month outpatient follow-up: patient doing well + healed ileostomy + nutrition stable + outpatient ID antibiotic course completed + ileostomy education ongoing + adult daughter supportive + family well + couples therapy continuing + insurance + community resources + ileostomy reversal evaluation scheduled at 6 months if recovery continues + multidisciplinary follow-up + meaningful long-term recovery
opts.rat: Standard 3-month outpatient trajectory + comprehensive recovery + family + multidisciplinary follow-up + reversal evaluation. Foundation for long-term outcome + potential restoration of bowel continuity.

opts.text: 1-year follow-up: ileostomy reversal completed at 6 months post-OR + successful + bowel continuity restored; patient returned to retirement activities + travel + family + grandchildren; ongoing PCP + colorectal + diabetes follow-up; lifestyle modifications + weight loss + diet + activity + adherence; gratitude for medical team + saved life + comprehensive care; meaningful long-term recovery + advocacy for sepsis recognition + post-op care
opts.rat: Excellent 1-year outcome — successful reversal + restoration of function + meaningful life + family + advocacy. Demonstrates value of comprehensive RRT + Surviving Sepsis bundle + source control + multidisciplinary care + family engagement. Standard ideal long-term trajectory.
